_meta: Settle config. Synthetic. Tolerances, confidence routing, claim rules, and the seeded supplier mapping. Deterministic code reads thresholds from here; the LLM reads the rule descriptions as context, not as code.

confidence_routing.note: Discrepancies classified at >= auto_act_threshold and within policy are acted on automatically; everything else is escalated to a human with the drafted action attached.

policy_limits.note: A claim/debit above this value is always escalated regardless of confidence (blast-radius control).

tolerances.note: Any non-zero received-vs-ordered delta is a discrepancy. (Tolerance kept at 0 for the demo to make the case crisp; configurable per partner in production.)

in_transit_damage.trigger: Damage corroborated by BOTH the carrier POD (damage noted at delivery) AND the GRN damage inspection.

supplier_short_load.trigger: Cases short at receipt WHILE the POD shows full pallet count delivered with no shortage noted (i.e., loss did not occur in carrier custody at pallet level) -> inference of under-filled pallets / short load by supplier.

carrier_loss.trigger: POD itself notes a pallet/unit shortage at delivery (loss in carrier custody).

data_error.trigger: Discrepancy attributable to a document/keying error rather than physical goods.

supplier_mapping._meta: Seeded cross-reference for the one supplier in the demo. In production this is a maintained, human-confirmed mapping table (the real engineering cost).
